On July 10, 1940, the incredibly massive, fiercely intense, and absolutely decisive Battle of Britain officially and aggressively commenced during World War II. The massive German Luftwaffe aggressively launched an unrelenting, massive aerial bombardment campaign against the United Kingdom, attempting to absolutely shatter the Royal Air Force (RAF) to enable a massive ground invasion. The incredible, massive heroism of British fighter pilots completely defeated the German air superiority, marking the absolute, very first major defeat of Hitler's military forces.

On July 10, 1985, a massive, highly controversial, and incredibly scandalous international geopolitical incident violently occurred in Auckland, New Zealand. Agents of the French foreign intelligence service (DGSE) aggressively and secretly bombed and sank the *Rainbow Warrior*, the massive flagship of the Greenpeace environmental organization. The incredibly massive, lethal sabotage was fiercely designed to prevent the ship from heavily protesting massive French nuclear testing in the Pacific, deeply humiliating the French government and fiercely outraging the global environmental movement.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On July 10, 1949, Sunil Gavaskar, the absolutely massive, incredibly brilliant, and universally revered legend of Indian cricket, was officially born in Bombay (Mumbai). A highly masterful, incredibly disciplined opening batsman, he aggressively achieved absolute sporting immortality by becoming the very first cricketer in massive global history to score 10,000 Test runs and aggressively break Don Bradman's massive record for Test centuries, heavily cementing his legacy as a massive, foundational pillar of India's global sporting prestige.

On July 10, 1806, the incredibly massive, brutally violent, and highly historic Vellore Mutiny officially and aggressively erupted in the Madras Presidency. Indian sepoys of the British East India Company, fiercely enraged by massive, culturally insensitive changes to their military dress codes that heavily offended both Hindu and Islamic religious sentiments, aggressively mutinied, brutally slaughtering over 100 British troops. While rapidly and massively crushed, it served as the absolute, terrifying, and massive precursor to the devastating Indian Rebellion of 1857.
